evidence: Abuse in the context of criticism resonates most with destructive symmetry (4), ethical value (4), relational ontology (5), and catalytic interconnection (2). It ties to individual scale (3) and the lived experience of criticism (3). Lower scores reflect its focus on interpersonal dynamics and moral implications without broader existential or structural impacts.

The word 'abuser' strongly resonates with destructive symmetry (10) as the dark side of criticism. It deeply connects to relational ontology (9) due to the inherent power dynamics between abuser and victim. Ethical value (9) reflects moral judgment central to criticism. Identification (8) and semantic meaning (7) highlight naming and condemning the role. Catalytic interconnection (4) ties to how abuse triggers disapproval, while essential resonance (8) captures the core essence of harmful dynamics.

Abusive criticism strongly resonates with destructive symmetry (10), ethical value (8), and relational ontology (7). It operates at individual/community scales (7/4) and creates essential/resonant connections (7/6). Its permanence and cyclical aspects are minimal.

Admonish, as a form of criticism, strongly resonates with relational dynamics (ontological.relational 8), individual focus (scale.individual 8), and ethical value (value_generation.ethical 7). It also connects to catalyzing change (interconnection.catalytic 4) and structural aspects of meaning (ontological.semantic 6).

Admonishment strongly resonates with individual scale (8), relational ontology (7), and ethical value (8) because it is a personal, interpersonal critique rooted in moral judgment. It also aligns with semantic ontology (8) as it carries explicit meaning about disapproval. Scores for destructive symmetry (6) reflect its critical nature opposing praise. Lower scores reflect its lack of systemic scale or existential resonance.

Admonition resonates most with relational dynamics (directed criticism between parties), ethical value (judgment of right/wrong), and superficial resonance (surface-level disapproval). It operates at an individual scale and influences behavior through relational connections, while its ethical underpinning drives its functional utility in guiding conduct.

Admonitory criticism warns or guides toward improvement, resonating most with relational dynamics (critic/recipient), ethical value, and structural/semantic meaning. It catalyzes change (catalytic) and reflects destructive symmetry as part of SOC.criticize's disapproval core.

Anathema resonates strongly with ethical value, relational ontology, and destructive symmetry as it embodies severe moral condemnation. It operates at societal/community scales and catalyzes exclusion, touching essential resonance through its core role in defining taboos.

Anathematisation strongly resonates with destructive symmetry (8), reflecting its role as a harsh condemnation. Its semantic layer (7) underscores its focus on meaning and taboo.
